title: CUSTOS API – Federal government costs
text: CUSTOS API (https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/docs/custos/) provides cost data Federal Government Cost Portal (Portal de Custos Governo Federal). breaks costs six categories: active staff, retired staff, pensioners, depreciation, transfers, costs. parameters API optional — can call function without arguments retrieve full dataset.

title: CUSTOS API – Federal government costs
text: CUSTOS API slow. server default 250 rows per page; package raises 500 default (lowered 1000 0.2.1 upstream load balancer started cutting broader queries). Even 500-row pages, unfiltered queries routinely hit HTTP 504 timeouts. Always filter queries least: year month — year-queries single common cause 504s; always pin single month production work org_level1 + org_level2 (reduce specific organization) legal_nature (reduce legal nature category) max_rows (set hard cap testing) package retries automatically 504s (5 attempts progressive backoff). pagination fails first page, rows already fetched discarded — receive partial tibble attr(result, "partial") = TRUE attr(result, "last_page_error") describing failure. Always check attributes working broad queries.

title: CUSTOS API – Federal government costs
text: six functions share optional filters: SIORG codes automatically zero-padded: can pass 244, "244", "000244" — produce query.

title: CUSTOS API – Federal government costs
code: library(tesouror) library(dplyr)  # Step 1: Look up SIORG codes for the organization you want orgaos <- get_siorg_organizations(power_code = 1, sphere_code = 1) mec <- orgaos |> filter(sigla == "MEC")          # code 244 inep <- orgaos |> filter(sigla == "INEP")         # code 249  # Step 2: Query CUSTOS with org AND month filters (year-only is unsafe!) # Active staff costs for INEP, June 2023 ativos_inep <- get_costs_active_staff(   year = 2023, month = 6,   org_level1 = 244,     # MEC — auto-padded to "000244"   org_level2 = 249      # INEP — auto-padded to "000249" )  # Always check whether pagination completed; on 504 mid-stream the # package returns a partial tibble rather than dropping the data. if (isTRUE(attr(ativos_inep, "partial"))) {   message("Partial result — last page failed: ",           attr(ativos_inep, "last_page_error")) }  # Pensioner costs for INEP, June 2023 only pensionistas_inep <- get_costs_pensioners(   year = 2023, month = 6,   org_level1 = 244,   org_level2 = 249 )  # Quick test: just grab the first 100 rows sample <- get_costs_active_staff(   year = 2023, month = 6,   legal_nature = 3,   max_rows = 100 )

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: tesouror package provides unified R interface Brazilian National Treasury (Tesouro Nacional) open data APIs. covers six major data sources: functions return tidy tibbles, use -memory caching, Portuguese-named (matching API) English-named versions.

title: Getting started with tesouror
code: # From CRAN (when available): install.packages("tesouror")  # Development version from GitHub: # remotes::install_github("StrategicProjects/tesouror")

title: Getting started with tesouror
code: library(tesouror)  # List all government entities entes <- get_entes()  # Get RREO data for Tocantins (IBGE code 17) rreo <- get_rreo(   an_exercicio = 2022, nr_periodo = 6,   co_tipo_demonstrativo = "RREO",   no_anexo = "RREO-Anexo 01",   co_esfera = "E", id_ente = 17 )  # Same query using English aliases rreo <- get_budget_report(   fiscal_year = 2022, period = 6,   report_type = "RREO",   appendix = "RREO-Anexo 01",   sphere = "E", entity_id = 17 )  # Federal government costs (always filter by org to avoid slow queries!) custos <- get_custos_pessoal_ativo(   ano = 2023, mes = 6,   organizacao_n1 = 244,  # MEC (auto-padded)   organizacao_n2 = 249   # INEP )  # Constitutional transfers (codes are Treasury-internal, NOT IBGE!) estados <- get_tc_estados() pe_code <- estados$codigo[estados$nome == "Pernambuco"] tc <- get_tc_por_estados(p_estado = pe_code, p_ano = 2023)  # Education spending data from SIOPE indicadores <- get_siope_indicators(year = 2023, period = 6, state = "PE")

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: functions cache responses -memory R session. means repeated calls parameters instantaneous. clear cache:
code: tesouror_clear_cache()

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: Every function two versions. Portuguese version uses exact API parameter names, English version uses descriptive English names: call endpoint return data. See vignette("siconfi"), vignette("custos"), vignette("sadipem"), vignette("siorg"), vignette("transferencias"), vignette("siope") articles API-specific details.
code: # Portuguese (API-native) get_dca(an_exercicio = 2022, id_ente = 17)  # English get_annual_accounts(fiscal_year = 2022, entity_id = 17)

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: Every function verbose parameter prints full API URL called. useful debugging testing browser/curl:
code: # Per call: get_costs_active_staff(year = 2023, month = 6, org_level1 = 244, verbose = TRUE) #> ℹ API call: https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/ords/custos/tt/pessoal_ativo?ano=2023&mes=6&organizacao_n1=000244&limit=1000  # Or globally for the session: options(tesouror.verbose = TRUE) get_entes()  # will print the URL options(tesouror.verbose = FALSE)  # turn off

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: ORDS-based APIs (SICONFI, CUSTOS, SADIPEM) return paginated results. page_size parameter controls many rows per page:
code: # CUSTOS defaults to 1000 rows/page (server default is only 250) custos <- get_costs_active_staff(   year = 2023, org_level1 = 244, org_level2 = 249 )  # Lower for quick tests: custos_sample <- get_costs_active_staff(   year = 2023, org_level1 = 244, org_level2 = 249,   page_size = 100, max_rows = 200 )  # SICONFI/SADIPEM default to server's 5000 rows/page (fast) entes <- get_entes()

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: API responses cleaned janitor::clean_names() ensure consistent snake_case column names (e.g., CO_IBGE becomes co_ibge).

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: Base URL: https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/ords/siconfi/tt/ Fiscal reports (RREO, RGF, DCA), accounting matrices (MSC), entity registry. Maintained STN. ORDS pagination (hasMore/offset) server default 5,000 rows/page. 18 functions (9 PT + 9 EN).

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: Base URL: https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/ords/custos/tt/ Cost data active/retired staff, pensioners, depreciation, transfers, costs. ORDS pagination default 1,000 rows/page (server default 250 slow; 5,000 causes timeouts). SIORG codes auto-padded (244 → "000244"). 12 functions (6 PT + 6 EN). Warning: Always filter organization level (organizacao_n1 + organizacao_n2) avoid downloading hundreds thousands rows.

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: Base URL: https://apiapex.tesouro.gov.br/aria/v1/transferencias_constitucionais/custom/ Constitutional transfers (FPE, FPM, FUNDEB, etc.). pagination (single response). Accepts vectors (c(1,2)) colon-separated strings ("1:2"). Uses Treasury-internal codes, IBGE. 14 functions (7 PT + 7 EN).

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: Base URL: https://estruturaorganizacional.dados.gov.br/ Federal organizational structure: ministries, autarchies, foundations. Used dictionary CUSTOS organization codes. pagination. 6 functions (3 PT + 3 EN).

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: Base URL: https://www.fnde.gov.br/olinda-ide/servico/DADOS_ABERTOS_SIOPE/versao/v1/odata/ Education spending data FNDE/MEC: revenues, expenses, indicators, staff compensation. OData pagination ($top/$skip) default 1,000 rows/page. Supports server-side filter (OData $filter), orderby, select. 16 functions (8 PT + 8 EN). Tip: Use filter = "NOM_MUNI eq 'Recife'" narrow results server. Column names filter/select/orderby must use original API names (uppercase). Run toupper(names(result)) max_rows = 1 query discover .

title: Getting started with tesouror
text: 80 functions share features: Retries: 5 attempts progressive backoff (3s, 6s, 9s, 12s, 15s) HTTP 500/502/503/504/429 connection failures. HTTP 400/404 retried. Caching: -memory per session. Clear tesouror_clear_cache(). verbose mode: Per call (verbose = TRUE) globally (options(tesouror.verbose = TRUE)). max_rows: Cap number rows returned (adjusts limit $top automatically). Column cleaning: janitor::clean_names() applied responses. Bilingual: Portuguese (API-native) English aliases every function. Error messages: Friendly, actionable messages URL hints. HTTP 400 errors suggest checking column names filter/select.

what: The problem: RREO labels drift across years
title: RREO longitudinal — handling layout drift across years
text: SICONFI RREO long table row identified appendix (no_anexo), account (conta) column (coluna). three drift across fiscal years even underlying concept stays : Appendix renaming. Federal social-security data General Regime (RGPS) lived RREO-Anexo 04.3 - RGPS 2015 2022 moved RREO-Anexo 04.4 - RGPS 2023 onwards. RPPS Civis FCDF moved Anexo 04.1 04.2 reform. Account-code shuffling. Account labels carry trailing Roman numerals tied appendix layout, indicator appears "Resultado Previdenciário RGPS (VII) = (III - VI)" one year "Resultado Previdenciário RGPS (V) = (III - IV)" another. Column suffix instability. given column shows "DESPESAS LIQUIDADAS ATÉ O BIMESTRE / 2019" 2019, "DESPESAS LIQUIDADAS ATÉ O BIMESTRE" (year) 2021–2022, "DESPESAS LIQUIDADAS ATÉ O BIMESTRE / 2023" 2023+, stray whitespace variants "... BIMESTRE/ 2023" (space) inside year. Together, three sources drift mean code filters SICONFI literal label values ends paste0() ladders full ifelse() special-case year. vignette shows helpers tesouror turn single function call.

title: RREO longitudinal — handling layout drift across years
text: rreo_layout() returns table inst/extdata/rreo_layout.csv maps (topic, regime, year_range) correct appendix year-stable matching key account label. Use directly look right appendix fetching data get_rreo():
code: library(tesouror)  rreo_layout() #> # A tibble: 7 × 8 #>   topic        regime     first_year last_year co_esfera no_anexo                conta_match                          indicador #>   <chr>        <chr>           <int>     <int> <chr>     <chr>                   <chr>                                <chr> #> 1 previdencia  civil_rpps       2015      2022 U         RREO-Anexo 04.1         resultado previdenciario rpps civis  resultado_previdenciario_civil #> 2 previdencia  civil_rpps       2023      2099 U         RREO-Anexo 04.2         resultado previdenciario rpps civis  resultado_previdenciario_civil #> 3 previdencia  fcdf             2015      2022 U         RREO-Anexo 04.1         resultado previdenciario - fcdf      resultado_previdenciario_fcdf #> 4 previdencia  fcdf             2023      2099 U         RREO-Anexo 04.2         resultado previdenciario - fcdf      resultado_previdenciario_fcdf #> 5 previdencia  militar          2015      2099 U         RREO-Anexo 04.2         resultado total - militares inativos resultado_militares_inativos #> 6 previdencia  rgps             2015      2022 U         RREO-Anexo 04.3 - RGPS  resultado previdenciario rgps        resultado_previdenciario_rgps #> 7 previdencia  rgps             2023      2099 U         RREO-Anexo 04.4 - RGPS  resultado previdenciario rgps        resultado_previdenciario_rgps layout <- rreo_layout() fetch_year <- function(year) {   rule <- layout[layout$topic == "previdencia" &                  layout$regime == "rgps" &                  year >= layout$first_year &                  year <= layout$last_year, ]   get_rreo(     an_exercicio = year, nr_periodo = 6,     co_tipo_demonstrativo = "RREO", no_anexo = rule$no_anexo[1],     co_esfera = "U", id_ente = 1   ) }

title: RREO longitudinal — handling layout drift across years
text: rreo_normalize_columns() strips trailing year suffixes ("/ 2019", "/2023", " /2023", "EM 2023") squishes whitespace, exposing year separately can distinguish current-year column previous-year comparator.
code: demo <- tibble::tibble(   coluna = c(     "DESPESAS LIQUIDADAS ATÉ O BIMESTRE / 2023",     "DESPESAS LIQUIDADAS ATÉ O BIMESTRE",     "DESPESAS LIQUIDADAS ATÉ O BIMESTRE/ 2018",     "INSCRITAS EM RESTOS A PAGAR NÃO PROCESSADOS EM 2023"   ) ) rreo_normalize_columns(demo) #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   coluna                                              coluna_padrao                              coluna_ano #>   <chr>                                               <chr>                                           <int> #> 1 DESPESAS LIQUIDADAS ATÉ O BIMESTRE / 2023           DESPESAS LIQUIDADAS ATÉ O BIMESTRE               2023 #> 2 DESPESAS LIQUIDADAS ATÉ O BIMESTRE                  DESPESAS LIQUIDADAS ATÉ O BIMESTRE                 NA #> 3 DESPESAS LIQUIDADAS ATÉ O BIMESTRE/ 2018            DESPESAS LIQUIDADAS ATÉ O BIMESTRE               2018 #> 4 INSCRITAS EM RESTOS A PAGAR NÃO PROCESSADOS EM 2023 INSCRITAS EM RESTOS A PAGAR NÃO PROCESSADOS      2023

title: RREO longitudinal — handling layout drift across years
text: tidy_rreo() runs rreo_normalize_columns() matches row’s conta year-appropriate rule rreo_layout(). Account labels matched stable stem (everything first (, accent-stripped lowercased), shifting Roman numerals break join. five-year deficit series RGPS, manual reconciliation appendix names, account codes, column suffixes.
code: library(dplyr)  # Pull the federal RGPS series for five years using the layout rgps_raw <- purrr::map_dfr(2019:2023, fetch_year)  rgps_tidy <- rgps_raw |>   tidy_rreo(topic = "previdencia", regime = "rgps")  panel <- rgps_tidy |>   filter(coluna_padrao == "DESPESAS LIQUIDADAS ATÉ O BIMESTRE",          is.na(coluna_ano) | coluna_ano == exercicio) |>   select(exercicio, indicador, regime, valor)  panel #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   exercicio indicador                     regime    valor #>       <int> <chr>                         <chr>     <dbl> #> 1      2019 resultado_previdenciario_rgps rgps   -2.13e11 #> 2      2020 resultado_previdenciario_rgps rgps   -2.61e11 #> 3      2021 resultado_previdenciario_rgps rgps   -2.48e11 #> 4      2022 resultado_previdenciario_rgps rgps   -2.67e11 #> 5      2023 resultado_previdenciario_rgps rgps   -3.13e11

title: RREO longitudinal — handling layout drift across years
text: Drop regime keep every regime registered topic :
code: all_topics <- rreo_layout() fetch_topic <- function(year, regime) {   rules <- all_topics[all_topics$topic == "previdencia" &                       all_topics$regime == regime &                       year >= all_topics$first_year &                       year <= all_topics$last_year, ]   if (nrow(rules) == 0L) return(NULL)   purrr::map_dfr(unique(rules$no_anexo), \(an) {     get_rreo(       an_exercicio = year, nr_periodo = 6,       co_tipo_demonstrativo = "RREO", no_anexo = an,       co_esfera = "U", id_ente = 1     )   }) }  regimes <- unique(all_topics$regime[all_topics$topic == "previdencia"]) raw_22_23 <- purrr::map_dfr(2022:2023, \(yr) {   purrr::map_dfr(regimes, \(rg) fetch_topic(yr, rg)) })  raw_22_23 |>   tidy_rreo(topic = "previdencia") |>   filter(coluna_padrao == "DESPESAS LIQUIDADAS ATÉ O BIMESTRE",          is.na(coluna_ano) | coluna_ano == exercicio) |>   select(exercicio, indicador, regime, valor) |>   distinct() #> # A tibble: 7 × 4 #>   exercicio indicador                      regime        valor #>       <int> <chr>                          <chr>         <dbl> #> 1      2022 resultado_previdenciario_civil civil_rpps -5.04e10 #> 2      2023 resultado_previdenciario_civil civil_rpps -5.47e10 #> 3      2022 resultado_previdenciario_fcdf  fcdf       -6.56e 9 #> 4      2023 resultado_previdenciario_fcdf  fcdf       -8.03e 9 #> 5      2022 resultado_militares_inativos   militar    -3.11e10 #> 6      2022 resultado_previdenciario_rgps  rgps       -2.67e11 #> 7      2023 resultado_previdenciario_rgps  rgps       -3.13e11

title: SADIPEM API – Public debt & credit operations
text: SADIPEM API (https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/docs/sadipem/) provides data Public Debt Verification Letters (PVL) — process subnational entities (states municipalities) request authorization credit operations debt management National Treasury.

title: SADIPEM API – Public debt & credit operations
text: usual workflow first search PVL requests, use id_pleito value results fetch detailed information. Important: id_pleito database ID (e.g., 40353), row number. Use pvl$id_pleito[1] get value first row, pvl$id_pleito[40353] (tries access row 40,353).
code: library(tesouror) library(dplyr)  # Step 1: Search PVLs for Pernambuco pvl_pe <- get_debt_requests(state = "PE")  # Step 2: Pick an id_pleito VALUE from the results id <- pvl_pe$id_pleito[1]  # Step 3: Get details using that id_pleito pagamentos <- get_credit_payment_schedule(request_id = id) cdp <- get_debt_capacity(request_id = id)

title: SADIPEM API – Public debt & credit operations
text: get_pvl_status() / get_pvl_tramitacao() function returns data approved (deferred) non-credit operation PVLs. Filter status == "Deferido" first:
code: pvl_deferidos <- pvl_pe |> filter(status == "Deferido") if (nrow(pvl_deferidos) > 0) {   status <- get_pvl_status(request_id = pvl_deferidos$id_pleito[1]) }

title: SICONFI API – Fiscal data
text: SICONFI API (https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/docs/siconfi/) provides fiscal data Brazilian states, municipalities, Federal District. includes budget execution reports (RREO), fiscal management reports (RGF), annual accounts declarations (DCA), accounting balances matrices (MSC).

title: SICONFI API – Fiscal data
code: library(tesouror) library(dplyr)  # All entities entes <- get_entes()  # Filter states only estados <- entes |> filter(esfera == "E")

title: SICONFI API – Fiscal data
code: # RREO Anexo 01 for Tocantins, 6th bimester of 2022 rreo <- get_budget_report(   fiscal_year = 2022, period = 6,   report_type = "RREO",   appendix = "RREO-Anexo 01",   sphere = "E", entity_id = 17 )

title: SIOPE API – Education spending data
text: SIOPE (Sistema de Informações sobre Orçamentos Públicos em Educação) provides education spending data states municipalities. system maintained FNDE (Fundo Nacional de Desenvolvimento da Educação) API uses OData-style interface hosted https://www.fnde.gov.br/olinda-ide/servico/DADOS_ABERTOS_SIOPE/. Data covers revenues, expenses, indicators, official contacts, staff compensation related public education spending.

title: SIOPE API – Education spending data
text: functions share three required parameters: Exception: get_siope_remuneracao() / get_siope_compensation() 4th required parameter: mes / month (1-12).

what: Performance tip: use filter to reduce downloads
title: SIOPE API – Education spending data
text: SIOPE API returns municipalities given state. single call São Paulo (state = "SP") returns 645+ rows. Use filter parameter apply server-side filtering download data need. much faster downloading everything filtering locally dplyr::filter().

previous_headings: Performance tip: use filter to reduce downloads
title: SIOPE API – Education spending data
code: # SLOW: downloads all 185 municipalities, then filters locally dados_pe <- get_siope_general_data(year = 2023, period = 6, state = "PE") recife <- dplyr::filter(dados_pe, nom_muni == "Recife")  # FAST: server returns only Recife's data recife <- get_siope_general_data(   year = 2023, period = 6, state = "PE",   filter = "NOM_MUNI eq 'Recife'" )  # Filter by IBGE code recife <- get_siope_general_data(   year = 2023, period = 6, state = "PE",   filter = "COD_MUNI eq 261160" )

title: SIOPE API – Education spending data
text: SIOPE API uses OData-style URL pattern different APIs package. Internally, package builds URLs like: {base} FNDE/MEC SIOPE OData root, available tesouror:::siope_base_url(). don’t need worry — just pass parameters package handles rest. Use verbose = TRUE see full URL:
code: {base}/Dados_Gerais_Siope(Ano_Consulta=@Ano_Consulta,Num_Peri=@Num_Peri,Sig_UF=@Sig_UF)   ?@Ano_Consulta=2023&@Num_Peri=6&@Sig_UF='PE'&$format=json get_siope_general_data(year = 2023, period = 6, state = "PE", verbose = TRUE) #> ℹ API call: https://www.fnde.gov.br/olinda-ide/servico/...

title: SIORG – Organizational Structure
text: SIORG API provides official organizational structure Brazilian Federal Executive Branch. SIORG codes identify ministries, agencies, departments, organizational units. matters tesouror: CUSTOS API uses SIORG codes organizacao_n1, organizacao_n2, organizacao_n3 parameters. SIORG functions serve dictionaries look codes.

title: SIORG – Organizational Structure
code: library(tesouror) library(dplyr)  # List all federal executive branch organizations orgaos <- get_siorg_organizations(power_code = 1, sphere_code = 1)  # Key columns: #   codigo_unidade     – SIORG code (use as organizacao_n1 in CUSTOS) #   codigo_unidade_pai – parent unit code #   nome               – organization name #   sigla              – abbreviation #   codigo_tipo_unidade – "orgao" or "entidade" #   codigo_natureza_juridica – 1=Empresa Pública, 2=Fundação, 3=Adm.Direta, 4=Autarquia  # Find the Ministry of Education mec <- orgaos |> filter(grepl("Educação", nome), codigo_tipo_unidade == "orgao") mec |> select(codigo_unidade, nome, sigla) # codigo_unidade = "244" → this is the org_level1 for CUSTOS

title: SIORG – Organizational Structure
text: codigo_unidade_pai column lets navigate hierarchy. Units whose codigo_unidade_pai matches ministry’s code direct children (N2 level). children N3.
code: # All organizations under MEC (direct children = N2) mec_children <- orgaos |>   filter(codigo_unidade_pai == "244") |>  # MEC's code   select(codigo_unidade, nome, sigla, codigo_tipo_unidade) mec_children # Examples: INEP (249), FNDE (253), CAPES (478), etc.  # For deeper structure, use get_siorg_structure() estrutura <- get_siorg_structure(unit_code = 244) estrutura |> select(codigo_unidade, codigo_unidade_pai, nome, sigla)

title: SIORG – Organizational Structure
text: SIORG returns plain codes like "244", CUSTOS API expects zero-padded 6-digit strings like "000244". package pads automatically — just pass SIORG code directly:
code: # Active staff costs for MEC — "244" is auto-padded to "000244" custos_mec <- get_costs_active_staff(   year = 2023,   org_level1 = 244  # numeric or character, both work )  # Drill down to INEP custos_inep <- get_costs_active_staff(   year = 2023,   org_level1 = 244,   org_level2 = 249  # auto-padded to "000249" )

title: SIORG – Organizational Structure
text: CUSTOS API uses SIORG codes 6-digit zero-padded strings (e.g., "000244"). package auto-pads plain codes, can pass 244, "244", "000244" — work. CUSTOS response also includes co_organizacao_n0 (top-level authority, e.g., Presidência da República = "000026"), query parameter appears results.

title: SIORG – Organizational Structure

title: Transferências Constitucionais API
text: Transferências Constitucionais API provides data constitutional transfers Federal Government states municipalities. transfers include FPE (Fundo de Participação dos Estados), FPM (Fundo de Participação dos Municípios), FUNDEB, mandatory transfers. Base URL: https://apiapex.tesouro.gov.br/aria/v1/transferencias_constitucionais/custom

title: Transferências Constitucionais API
text: functions use get_tc_ prefix. filter parameters use numeric codes, names abbreviations. Use lookup functions (get_tc_transferencias, get_tc_estados, get_tc_municipios) obtain codes. Multiple values separated colons (:) — e.g., p_estado = "1:2" Acre Alagoas.

title: Transferências Constitucionais API
text: filter parameters use numeric codes internal API (IBGE codes). Always look codes first.
code: library(tesouror)  # Transfer types — returns codigo + nome tipos <- get_tc_transfer_types() tipos # e.g., codigo=10 nome="FUNDEB", codigo=14 nome="AJUSTE FUNDEB"  # State codes — returns codigo + nome estados <- get_tc_states() estados # e.g., codigo=16 nome="Pernambuco" (NOT the IBGE code!)  # Municipality codes — filter by the state code from above pe_code <- estados$codigo[estados$nome == "Pernambuco"] mun_pe <- get_tc_municipalities(state_code = pe_code) mun_pe

title: Transferências Constitucionais API
code: # Use the state code obtained from get_tc_states() pe_code <- estados$codigo[estados$nome == "Pernambuco"]  # All transfers for Pernambuco in 2023 tc_pe <- get_tc_by_state(state_code = pe_code, year = 2023)  # FUNDEB + AJUSTE FUNDEB — pass a vector of transfer type codes tc_fundeb <- get_tc_by_state(   state_code = pe_code,   year = 2023,   transfer_type = c(10, 14) )  # Multiple states, Jan-Mar 2023 — vectors work everywhere ac_code <- estados$codigo[estados$nome == "Acre"] al_code <- estados$codigo[estados$nome == "Alagoas"] tc_multi <- get_tc_by_state(   state_code = c(ac_code, al_code),   year = 2023,   month = 1:3 )  # Same query with detailed breakdown tc_det <- get_tc_by_state_detail(   state_code = c(ac_code, al_code),   year = 2023,   month = 1:3 )

title: Transferências Constitucionais API
code: # Look up municipality codes pe_code <- estados$codigo[estados$nome == "Pernambuco"] mun_pe <- get_tc_municipalities(state_code = pe_code) recife_code <- mun_pe$codigo[mun_pe$nome == "Recife"]  # All municipalities in Pernambuco in 2023 tc_mun <- get_tc_by_municipality(state_code = pe_code, year = 2023)  # Specific municipality: Recife tc_recife <- get_tc_by_municipality_detail(   state_code = pe_code,   municipality = recife_code,   year = 2023 )

title: Transferências Constitucionais API
text: multi-value parameters accept either colon-separated string ("1:2:3") R vector (c(1, 2, 3)).

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
text: transferências constitucionais são repasses obrigatórios da União para estados e municípios, previstos na Constituição Federal. Representam principal fonte de receita corrente da maioria dos municípios brasileiros, financiando desde educação básica (FUNDEB) até compensações por desonerações tributárias. Pernambuco, com seus 185 municípios distribuídos em 12 Regiões de Desenvolvimento (RDs), oferece um caso rico para análise: Região Metropolitana de Recife concentra volume, mas o Sertão se destaca per capita. Nesta vinheta exploramos os dados da API de Transferências Constitucionais Tesouro Nacional usando o pacote tesouror. análise cobre: Categorização dos tipos de transferência em 5 grandes grupos Séries históricas anuais e mensais Análise per capita com dados populacionais SICONFI Mapas coropléticos por município e por Região de Desenvolvimento Análise regional comparativa entre 12 RDs

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
code: library(tesouror) library(tidyverse) library(patchwork) library(sf) library(scales) library(ggrepel) library(MetBrewer) library(glue)

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
text: função get_tc_by_municipality_detail() retorna os repasses com granularidade de subtipo (ex: FUNDEB/FPM, Royalties/ANP). Buscamos série completa de 2015 2026 para todos os municípios de Pernambuco. O pacote inclui malha municipal de PE com Regiões de Desenvolvimento, macrorregiões e população (Censo 2022):
code: tipos   <- get_tc_transfer_types() estados <- get_tc_states()  pe_code <- estados |>   filter(str_detect(nome, "(?i)pernambuco")) |>   pull(codigo)  anos <- 2015:2026  tc_pe_raw <- get_tc_by_municipality_detail(   state = pe_code,   year  = anos ) |>   rename(     ano           = an_distribuicao,     mes           = me_distribuicao,     transferencia = sg_detalhe,     valor         = total   ) pe_sf <- read_rds(system.file("data", "pernambuco_sf.rds", package = "tesouror"))  pe_lookup <- pe_sf |>   st_drop_geometry() |>   select(     ibge7, municipio,     rd           = regiao_de_desenvolvimento,     macrorregiao, mesorregiao, populacao   ) |>   mutate(co_ibge = as.integer(ibge7))  pe_lookup |> count(rd, sort = TRUE)

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
text: O endpoint _detail retorna nomes formato PRINCIPAL/subtipo. Normalizamos para o nome principal e agrupamos em 5 categorias:
code: ordem_cat <- c(   "Fundos de Participação",   "Educação",   "Compensações Tributárias",   "Royalties e Recursos Naturais",   "Transferências Especiais" )  tc_pe <- tc_pe_raw |>   mutate(     transferencia_detalhe = transferencia,     transferencia_principal = case_when(       str_starts(transferencia, "AJUSTE FUNDEB") ~ "AJUSTE FUNDEB",       str_starts(transferencia, "AFM/AFE")       ~ "AFM/AFE",       str_starts(transferencia, "CIDE")          ~ "CIDE-Combustíveis",       str_starts(transferencia, "Cessão")        ~ "Cessão Onerosa",       str_starts(transferencia, "FPM 1%")        ~ "FPM 1%",       str_starts(transferencia, "FPM")           ~ "FPM",       str_starts(transferencia, "FUNDEB")        ~ "FUNDEB",       str_starts(transferencia, "FEX")           ~ "FEX",       str_starts(transferencia, "IOF")           ~ "IOF-Ouro",       str_starts(transferencia, "ITR")           ~ "ITR",       str_starts(transferencia, "LC 173")        ~ "LC 173/2020 (PFEC)",       str_starts(transferencia, "LC 176")        ~ "LC 176/2020 (ADO25)",       str_starts(transferencia, "LC 201")        ~ "LC 201/2023",       str_starts(transferencia, "LC 87")         ~ "LC 87/96 (Lei Kandir)",       str_starts(transferencia, "Royalties")     ~ "Royalties",       TRUE                                       ~ transferencia     ),     categoria = factor(case_when(       transferencia_principal %in% c("FPM", "FPM 1%")         ~ "Fundos de Participação",       transferencia_principal %in% c("FUNDEB", "AJUSTE FUNDEB")         ~ "Educação",       transferencia_principal %in% c("CIDE-Combustíveis", "IOF-Ouro", "ITR",                                      "LC 87/96 (Lei Kandir)", "LC 176/2020 (ADO25)",                                      "LC 201/2023")         ~ "Compensações Tributárias",       transferencia_principal %in% c("Royalties", "Cessão Onerosa")         ~ "Royalties e Recursos Naturais",       transferencia_principal %in% c("FEX", "AFM/AFE", "LC 173/2020 (PFEC)")         ~ "Transferências Especiais",       TRUE ~ NA_character_     ), levels = ordem_cat),     data = make_date(ano, mes, 1L),     valor_milhoes = valor / 1e6   )

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
code: tc_pe <- tc_pe |>   left_join(pe_lookup, by = "co_ibge")  tc_mun_ano <- tc_pe |>   group_by(co_ibge, municipio, rd, macrorregiao, ano, categoria) |>   summarise(valor = sum(valor, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")  tc_mun_total <- tc_pe |>   group_by(co_ibge, municipio, rd, macrorregiao, ano, populacao) |>   summarise(valor_total = sum(valor, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>   mutate(valor_per_capita = valor_total / populacao)  ultimo_ano <- tc_mun_total |>   mutate(ano = as.integer(ano)) |>   filter(ano < max(ano)) |>   pull(ano) |>   max()  tc_geo <- pe_sf |>   mutate(co_ibge = as.integer(ibge7)) |>   left_join(     tc_mun_total |> filter(ano == ultimo_ano) |>       select(-macrorregiao, -municipio),     by = "co_ibge"   )  rd_bordas <- pe_sf |>   filter(municipio != "Fernando de Noronha") |>   group_by(regiao_de_desenvolvimento) |>   summarise(geometry = st_union(geometry), .groups = "drop")

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
text: Usamos paletas MetBrewer para consistência visual: Hokusai3 para 5 categorias de transferência e Klimt para 12 Regiões de Desenvolvimento.
code: pal_cat   <- met.brewer("Hokusai3", 5) cores_cat <- set_names(pal_cat, ordem_cat)  rd_nomes <- sort(unique(pe_lookup$rd)) pal_rd   <- met.brewer("Klimt", length(rd_nomes)) cores_rd <- set_names(pal_rd, rd_nomes)  tema_tc <- theme_light(base_size = 10) +   theme(     plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),     plot.subtitle    = element_text(color = "grey40"),     legend.position  = "bottom",     legend.title     = element_blank(),     panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),     strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold"),     axis.title.x     = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10)),     axis.title.y     = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10))   )  tema_mapa <- theme_void(base_size = 11) +   theme(     plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11, hjust = 0.5),     plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 9, hjust = 0.5,                                  margin = margin(b = 0, t = 5)),     plot.caption  = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 7, hjust = 1,                                  margin = margin(t = 10)),     legend.position = "bottom",     legend.title    = element_text(size = 9),     legend.text     = element_text(size = 8),     plot.margin     = margin(5, 5, 5, 5)   )  guia_barra <- guide_colorbar(   barwidth = 15, barheight = 0.5,   title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0.5 )  repel_mapa <- list(   size = 2.5, lineheight = 0.85, colour = "grey50",   fill = alpha("white", 0.9), label.size = 0.15,   label.padding = unit(0.2, "lines"),   force = 8, force_pull = 0.3, box.padding = 1.0, point.padding = 0.5,   segment.color = "grey30", segment.size = 0.3, segment.linetype = "solid",   arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.015, "npc"), type = "closed"),   min.segment.length = 0, max.overlaps = 20, seed = 42 )  # Helper: adiciona labels ggrepel com estilo padronizado dos mapas # (ggrepel não suporta !!! do rlang, então usamos do.call) label_mapa <- function(data, mapping) {   do.call(geom_label_repel, c(list(data = data, mapping = mapping), repel_mapa)) }

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
text: Fundos de Participação e Educação dominam, respondendo por mais de 80% total. O crescimento nominal é contínuo ao longo da série.
code: tc_anual_cat <- tc_pe |>   group_by(ano, categoria) |>   summarise(valor = sum(valor, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>   mutate(valor_bi = valor / 1e9)  p1 <- ggplot(tc_anual_cat, aes(ano, valor_bi, fill = categoria)) +   geom_col(width = 0.9, alpha = 0.9) +   scale_fill_manual(values = cores_cat) +   scale_y_continuous(     labels = label_number(suffix = " bi", decimal.mark = ","), expand = c(0, 0)   ) +   labs(     title = glue("Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de PE em {ultimo_ano}"),     subtitle = "Valores nominais anuais, em R$ bilhões",     x = "Ano", y = "Transferências"   ) +   scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) + tema_tc  p2 <- ggplot(tc_anual_cat, aes(ano, valor_bi, fill = categoria)) +   geom_col(position = "fill", width = 0.9, alpha = 0.9) +   scale_fill_manual(values = cores_cat) +   scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(), expand = c(0, 0)) +   labs(subtitle = "Composição percentual por categoria", x = NULL, y = NULL) +   scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) + tema_tc  p1 / p2 + plot_layout(guides = "collect") &   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
text: sazonalidade é marcante: dezembro e julho concentram repasses extraordinários (decêndios adicionais de FPM e complementação FUNDEB).
code: tc_mensal_cat <- tc_pe |>   group_by(data, categoria) |>   summarise(valor = sum(valor, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>   mutate(valor_milhoes = valor / 1e6)  tc_mensal_cat |>   filter(     data < max(data),     !categoria %in% c("Royalties e Recursos Naturais", "Transferências Especiais")   ) |>   ggplot(aes(data, valor_milhoes, color = categoria)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.5) +   geom_point(size = 0.5) +   facet_wrap(~categoria, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +   scale_color_manual(values = cores_cat) +   scale_x_date(date_labels = "%Y", date_breaks = "1 year") +   scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ",")) +   labs(title = "Principais Séries Mensais por Categoria",        subtitle = "Valores nominais em R$ milhões",        x = "Ano", y = "Transferências em R$ milhões") +   tema_tc +   theme(legend.position = "none",         strip.background = element_rect(fill = "black", color = NA),         strip.text = element_text(color = "white", face = "bold"))

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
text: Os maiores centros urbanos da RMR lideram em valores absolutos.
code: top10_abs <- tc_mun_total |>   mutate(ano = as.integer(ano)) |>   filter(ano == ultimo_ano) |>   slice_max(valor_total, n = 10)  ggplot(top10_abs, aes(valor_total / 1e6, fct_reorder(municipio, valor_total))) +   geom_col(fill = "#2E86AB", width = 0.7) +   geom_text(     aes(label = paste0(       "R$", format(round(valor_total / 1e6, 2), big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ","), " mi"     )),     hjust = -0.1, size = 2.75   ) +   scale_x_continuous(     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.3)),     breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 10),     labels = label_number(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ",")   ) +   labs(     title    = glue("Top 10 Municípios — Valor Absoluto ({ultimo_ano})"),     subtitle = "Total de transferências constitucionais em R$ milhões",     x = "Valores em R$ milhões", y = NULL   ) +   scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +   tema_tc +   theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
text: normalização por população inverte o ranking: municípios pequenos Sertão recebem proporcionalmente mais por habitante — o coeficiente FPM favorece municípios com menor população.

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
code: top10_pc <- tc_mun_total |>   mutate(ano = as.integer(ano)) |>   filter(ano == ultimo_ano, !is.na(valor_per_capita)) |>   slice_max(valor_per_capita, n = 10)  bot10_pc <- tc_mun_total |>   mutate(ano = as.integer(ano)) |>   filter(ano == ultimo_ano, !is.na(valor_per_capita)) |>   slice_min(valor_per_capita, n = 10)  p_top <- ggplot(top10_pc, aes(valor_per_capita, fct_reorder(municipio, valor_per_capita))) +   geom_col(fill = "#2E86AB", width = 0.7) +   geom_text(     aes(label = paste0("R$ ", format(round(valor_per_capita, 0), big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ","))),     hjust = -0.1, size = 3   ) +   scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.35))) +   scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +   labs(title = "Top 10: Maior per capita", x = "R$ por habitante", y = NULL) +   tema_tc + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 9)) +   theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())  p_bot <- ggplot(bot10_pc, aes(valor_per_capita, fct_reorder(stringr::str_wrap(municipio, 12), valor_per_capita))) +   geom_col(fill = "#C73E1D", width = 0.7) +   geom_text(     aes(label = paste0("R$ ", format(round(valor_per_capita, 0), big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ","))),     hjust = -0.1, size = 3   ) +   scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.35))) +   scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +   labs(title = "Bottom 10: Menor per capita",        x = "R$ por habitante", y = NULL) +   tema_tc + theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 9))  +   theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())  p_top + p_bot +   plot_annotation(     title    = "Transferências Constitucionais Per Capita",     subtitle = "R$ por habitante"   )

what: 6.2 Evolução per capita ao longo do tempo
title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
code: tc_anual_pc <- tc_mun_total |>   filter(!is.na(populacao)) |>   group_by(ano) |>   summarise(valor_total = sum(valor_total, na.rm = TRUE),             populacao = sum(populacao, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>   mutate(per_capita = valor_total / populacao, ano = as.integer(ano)) |>   filter(ano < max(ano))  ggplot(tc_anual_pc, aes(ano, per_capita)) +   geom_line(color = "#2E86AB", linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(color = "#2E86AB", size = 2.5) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = function(x) seq(min(x), max(x), by = 1),                      labels = label_number(big.mark = "", accuracy = 1)) +   scale_y_continuous(     labels = label_number(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ","),     breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 10)) +   labs(title = "Transferências Per Capita: Total PE ao Longo do Tempo",        subtitle = "R$ por habitante/ano", x = "Ano", y = "R$ per capita") +   tema_tc

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
text: mapa absoluto RMR domina. per capita, os municípios Sertão se destacam. escala logarítmica absoluto é essencial: Recife recebe ~50x mais que municípios pequenos.
code: tc_geo_cont <- tc_geo |> filter(municipio != "Fernando de Noronha") bbox <- st_bbox(tc_geo_cont)  centroides_abs <- tc_geo_cont |>   st_centroid() |> slice_max(valor_total, n = 8, na_rm = TRUE) |>   mutate(x = st_coordinates(geometry)[, 1], y = st_coordinates(geometry)[, 2],          label = paste0(municipio, "\nR$ ", round(valor_total / 1e6, 0), " mi"))  centroides_pc <- tc_geo_cont |>   st_centroid() |> slice_max(valor_per_capita, n = 8, na_rm = TRUE) |>   mutate(x = st_coordinates(geometry)[, 1], y = st_coordinates(geometry)[, 2],          label = paste0(municipio, "\nR$ ", format(round(valor_per_capita, 0),                                                     big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ","))) ggplot() +   geom_sf(data = tc_geo_cont, aes(fill = valor_total / 1e6),           color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.05) +   geom_sf(data = rd_bordas, fill = NA, color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.4) +   coord_sf(xlim = c(bbox["xmin"] - 0.05, bbox["xmax"] + 0.05),            ylim = c(bbox["ymin"] - 0.05, bbox["ymax"] + 0.25), expand = FALSE) +   label_mapa(centroides_abs, aes(x = x, y = y, label = label)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(     option = "inferno", name = "R$ milhões", trans = "log10",     labels = label_number(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ","),     breaks = c(1, 5, 10, 50, 200, 1000),     na.value = "grey90", direction = -1, guide = guia_barra) +   labs(title = "Transferências Constitucionais — Valor Absoluto",        subtitle = glue("Total {ultimo_ano} (R$ milhões, escala log)"),        caption = "Fonte: Tesouro Nacional/tesouror") +   tema_mapa ggplot() +   geom_sf(data = tc_geo_cont |> filter(!is.na(valor_per_capita)),           aes(fill = valor_per_capita), color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.05) +   geom_sf(data = rd_bordas, fill = NA, color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.4) +   coord_sf(xlim = c(bbox["xmin"] - 0.05, bbox["xmax"] + 0.05),            ylim = c(bbox["ymin"] - 0.05, bbox["ymax"] + 0.25), expand = FALSE) +   label_mapa(centroides_pc, aes(x = x, y = y, label = label)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(     option = "mako", name = "R$ per capita", direction = -1, na.value = "grey90",     labels = label_number(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ","), guide = guia_barra) +   labs(title = "Transferências Constitucionais — Per Capita",        subtitle = glue("Total {ultimo_ano} (R$ por habitante)"),        caption = "Malha: IBGE | População: SICONFI") +   tema_mapa

what: 6.4 Mapa de referência: Regiões de Desenvolvimento
title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
code: rd_bordas_proj <- rd_bordas |> st_transform(31985) bbox_bordas    <- st_bbox(rd_bordas_proj)  centroides_bordas <- rd_bordas_proj |>   st_centroid() |>   mutate(x = st_coordinates(geometry)[, 1], y = st_coordinates(geometry)[, 2],          label = regiao_de_desenvolvimento)  ggplot(rd_bordas_proj) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = regiao_de_desenvolvimento), color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +   geom_label_repel(     data = centroides_bordas, aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),     size = 2.5, lineheight = 0.85, colour = "grey20",     fill = alpha("white", 0.85), label.size = 0.15,     label.padding = unit(0.15, "lines"),     segment.color = "grey40", segment.size = 0.3,     min.segment.length = 0.2, seed = 42, max.overlaps = 15) +   scale_fill_manual(values = cores_rd) +   coord_sf(xlim = c(bbox_bordas["xmin"] - 5000, bbox_bordas["xmax"] + 5000),            ylim = c(bbox_bordas["ymin"], bbox_bordas["ymax"] + 15000),            expand = FALSE) +   labs(title = "Regiões de Desenvolvimento de Pernambuco",        subtitle = "12 RDs definidas pela CONDEPE/FIDEM") +   tema_mapa + theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
text: Metropolitana concentra o maior volume, seguida pelo Agreste Central (polo de Caruaru) e Mata Sul.
code: tc_rd <- tc_pe |> filter(!is.na(rd))  tc_rd_anual <- tc_rd |>   group_by(ano, rd) |>   summarise(valor = sum(valor, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>   mutate(valor_bi = valor / 1e9)  ggplot(tc_rd_anual, aes(ano, valor_bi, fill = rd)) +   geom_col(width = 0.7) +   scale_fill_manual(values = cores_rd) +   scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(suffix = " bi", decimal.mark = ","),                      breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 10),                      expand = c(0, 0)) +   labs(title = "Transferências por Região de Desenvolvimento",        subtitle = "Valores nominais anuais, em R$ bilhões", x = NULL, y = "R$") +   tema_tc + guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE))

what: 7.2 Composição por categoria dentro de cada RD
title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
code: tc_rd_cat <- tc_rd |>   filter(ano == ultimo_ano) |>   group_by(rd, categoria) |>   summarise(valor = sum(valor, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>   mutate(valor_mi = valor / 1e6)  ggplot(tc_rd_cat, aes(valor_mi, fct_reorder(rd, valor_mi, .fun = sum), fill = categoria)) +   geom_col(width = 0.7) +   scale_fill_manual(values = cores_cat) +   scale_x_continuous(     labels = label_number(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ","),     breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 10),     expand = c(0,0)) +   labs(title = glue("Composição por RD — {ultimo_ano}"),        subtitle = "R$ milhões, por categoria de transferência", x = "R$ milhões", y = NULL) +   tema_tc

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
text: RDs Sertão (Moxotó, Pajeú, Araripe) recebem significativamente mais per capita que Metropolitana.
code: rd_pop <- pe_lookup |> group_by(rd) |> summarise(populacao = sum(populacao))  tc_rd_pc <- tc_rd |>   filter(ano == ultimo_ano) |>   group_by(rd) |>   summarise(n_mun = n_distinct(co_ibge), valor_total = sum(valor, na.rm = TRUE),             .groups = "drop") |>   left_join(rd_pop, by = "rd") |>   mutate(per_capita = valor_total / populacao)  ggplot(tc_rd_pc, aes(per_capita, fct_reorder(rd, per_capita))) +   geom_col(aes(fill = rd), width = 0.8, show.legend = FALSE) +   geom_text(aes(label = paste0("R$ ", format(round(per_capita, 0),                                               big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ","),                                 "  (", n_mun, " mun.)")),             hjust = -0.05, size = 3) +   scale_fill_manual(values = cores_rd) +   scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.35))) +   labs(     title    = "Transferência Per Capita por Região de Desenvolvimento",     subtitle = glue("{ultimo_ano} R$ por habitante"),     x = "R$ por habitante", y = NULL   ) +   tema_tc +   theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
text: distância entre Sertão e Metropolitana se mantém relativamente estável ao longo da década — o mecanismo redistributivo não está reduzindo disparidade per capita.
code: tc_rd_pc_serie <- tc_rd |>   group_by(ano, rd) |>   summarise(valor_total = sum(valor, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>   left_join(rd_pop, by = "rd") |>   mutate(per_capita = valor_total / populacao, ano = as.integer(ano)) |>   filter(ano < max(ano))  ggplot(tc_rd_pc_serie, aes(ano, per_capita, color = rd)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) + geom_point(size = 1.2) +   scale_color_manual(values = cores_rd) +     scale_y_continuous(     labels = label_number(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ","),     breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 8)) +   scale_x_continuous(     breaks = function(x) seq(min(x), max(x), by = 1),     labels = label_number(big.mark = "", accuracy = 1),     expand = c(0.01,0.01)   ) +   labs(     title    = "Evolução das Transferências Per Capita por RD",     subtitle = "R$ por habitante/ano",     x = "Ano", y = "R$ per capita"   ) +   tema_tc +   guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE))

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
code: tc_rd_pc_geo <- rd_bordas |>   left_join(tc_rd_pc, by = c("regiao_de_desenvolvimento" = "rd"))  centroides_rd <- tc_rd_pc_geo |>   st_centroid() |>   mutate(x = st_coordinates(geometry)[, 1], y = st_coordinates(geometry)[, 2],          label = paste0(regiao_de_desenvolvimento, "\nR$ ",                         format(round(per_capita, 1), big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ",")))  ggplot() +   geom_sf(data = tc_rd_pc_geo, aes(fill = per_capita),           color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +   label_mapa(centroides_rd, aes(x = x, y = y, label = label)) +   coord_sf(xlim = c(bbox["xmin"] - 0.05, bbox["xmax"] + 0.05),            ylim = c(bbox["ymin"] - 0.05, bbox["ymax"] + 0.25), expand = FALSE) +   scale_fill_distiller(palette = "YlGnBu", direction = 1, name = "R$ per capita",                        labels = label_number(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ","),                        guide = guia_barra) +   labs(title = "Transferências Per Capita por Região de Desenvolvimento",        subtitle = glue("{ultimo_ano} — R$ por habitante/ano"),        caption = "Fonte: Tesouro Nacional/tesouror | CONDEPE/FIDEM") +   tema_mapa

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
text: Cada painel mostra distribuição espacial de uma das 5 categorias. Note como os Royalties se concentram em poucos municípios, enquanto o FPM tem distribuição mais homogênea.
code: tc_cat_geo <- tc_mun_ano |>   filter(ano == ultimo_ano) |>   right_join(pe_sf |> mutate(co_ibge = as.integer(ibge7)) |> select(co_ibge, geometry),              by = "co_ibge") |>   st_as_sf() |>   mutate(valor_mi = valor / 1e6) |>   filter(municipio != "Fernando de Noronha")  ggplot(tc_cat_geo) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = valor_mi), color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.03) +   geom_sf(data = rd_bordas, fill = NA, color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.3) +   facet_wrap(~categoria, ncol = 2) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(     option = "mako", name = "R$ mi", trans = "log10",     labels = label_number(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ","),     na.value = "grey90", direction = -1, guide = guia_barra) +   labs(title = "Distribuição Espacial por Categoria de Transferência",        subtitle = glue("{ultimo_ano} — escala logarítmica"),        caption = "Fonte: Tesouro Nacional/tesouror") +   tema_mapa + theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9))

title: Transferências Constitucionais para Municípios de Pernambuco
text: FPM e FUNDEB dominam: juntos representam mais de 80% total, com sazonalidade marcante (picos em julho e dezembro). O mapa muda radicalmente com per capita: grandes centros dominam em absoluto, mas o Sertão se destaca por habitante. 12 RDs revelam desigualdades persistentes: distância per capita entre Sertão e Metropolitana não se reduziu na década. Royalties são altamente concentrados: apenas alguns municípios recebem valores significativos.

title: Access Brazilian National Treasury Open Data APIs
text: tesouror provides unified R interface Brazilian National Treasury (Tesouro Nacional) open data APIs. covers five major data sources: functions return tidy tibbles Portuguese (matching API parameter names) English aliases.

title: Access Brazilian National Treasury Open Data APIs
code: # From CRAN (when available): install.packages("tesouror")  # Development version: # remotes::install_github("StrategicProjects/tesouror")

title: Access Brazilian National Treasury Open Data APIs
code: library(tesouror)  # List government entities entes <- get_entes()  # RREO for Tocantins rreo <- get_budget_report(   fiscal_year = 2022, period = 6, report_type = "RREO",   appendix = "RREO-Anexo 01", sphere = "E", entity_id = 17 )  # Federal government active staff costs (always filter by org AND month! # the CUSTOS backend is slow; year-wide queries often hit HTTP 504) custos <- get_costs_active_staff(   year = 2023, month = 6,   org_level1 = 244, org_level2 = 249  # MEC > INEP ) # If pagination fails mid-way the package returns a partial result; # check `attr(custos, "partial")` and `attr(custos, "last_page_error")`.  # Constitutional transfers (codes are Treasury-internal, NOT IBGE!) estados <- get_tc_states() pe <- estados$codigo[estados$nome == "Pernambuco"] tc <- get_tc_by_state(state_code = pe, year = 2023)  # Public debt requests for PE pvl <- get_debt_requests(state = "PE")  # SIORG: look up organization codes for CUSTOS queries orgaos <- get_siorg_organizations(power_code = 1, sphere_code = 1) # Use orgaos$codigo_unidade as org_level1 in get_costs_active_staff()  # SIOPE: education spending data indicadores <- get_siope_indicators(year = 2023, period = 6, state = "PE")  # Clear the cache if needed tesouror_clear_cache()

title: Access Brazilian National Treasury Open Data APIs
text: Bilingual: Portuguese English function/parameter names Caching: -memory cache avoids repeated API calls Pagination: Automatic handling multi-page responses progress verbose mode: Print full API URLs debugging (verbose = TRUE options(tesouror.verbose = TRUE)) page_size control: Tune rows per page speed vs. stability Clean output: janitor::clean_names() ensures consistent snake_case columns Friendly errors: Retries informative messages; NA validation required params Tidy output: results tibbles

title: Get other cost data — get_custos_demais
text: ano Integer. Year record. Optional. mes Integer. Month record (1-12). Optional. natureza_juridica Integer. Legal nature organization: 1 (Public Company), 2 (Public Foundation), 3 (Direct Administration), 4 (Autarchy), 6 (Mixed Economy Company). Optional. organizacao_n1 Character integer. SIORG code top-level organization (Ministry level). Use get_siorg_orgaos() look codes. can pass plain codes (e.g., 244) — automatically zero-padded 6-digit format API expects ("000244"). Optional. organizacao_n2 Character integer. SIORG code second-level organization. Use get_siorg_estrutura() browse sub-units. Automatically zero-padded. Optional. organizacao_n3 Character integer. SIORG code third-level organization. Use get_siorg_estrutura() browse sub-units. Automatically zero-padded. Optional. use_cache Logical. TRUE (default), uses -memory cache. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints full API URL called. Useful debugging testing browser. Defaults FALSE. page_size Integer. Number rows per API page. Defaults 500. CUSTOS backend slow broad queries: smaller pages robust HTTP 504 timeouts cost extra round-trips. server default 250 conservative; 1000+ may time . package returns attr(result, "partial") = TRUE, lower add mes filter. max_rows Numeric. Maximum number rows return. Defaults Inf (rows). Useful quick tests large datasets (e.g., max_rows = 100). year Integer. Year record. Optional. Maps ano. month Integer. Month record (1-12). Optional. Maps mes. legal_nature Integer. Legal nature organization: 1 (Public Company), 2 (Public Foundation), 3 (Direct Administration), 4 (Autarchy), 6 (Mixed Economy Company). Optional. Maps natureza_juridica. org_level1 Character integer. SIORG code top-level organization (Ministry level). Use get_siorg_organizations() look codes. Plain codes (e.g., 244) auto-padded "000244". Optional. Maps organizacao_n1. org_level2 Character integer. SIORG code (second level). Use get_siorg_structure() browse sub-units. Auto-padded. Optional. Maps organizacao_n2. org_level3 Character integer. SIORG code (third level). Use get_siorg_structure() browse sub-units. Auto-padded. Optional. Maps organizacao_n3.

what: tesouror: Access Brazilian National Treasury Open Data APIs — tesouror-package
title: tesouror: Access Brazilian National Treasury Open Data APIs — tesouror-package
text: Provides unified interface access open data Brazilian National Treasury (Tesouro Nacional) related government APIs. Covers six data sources: SICONFI https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/docs/siconfi/ fiscal reports (RREO, RGF, DCA, MSC) entity information; CUSTOS https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/docs/custos/ federal government cost data; SADIPEM https://apidatalake.tesouro.gov.br/docs/sadipem/ public debt credit operations; Transferencias Constitucionais https://apiapex.tesouro.gov.br/aria/v1/transferencias_constitucionais/docs constitutional transfers states municipalities; SIORG https://estruturaorganizacional.dados.gov.br federal organizational structure; SIOPE (FNDE/MEC) education spending data. Features automatic pagination, -memory caching, retry logic, tidy output.

title: tesouror 0.2.1
text: ords_fetch_all() now returns partial results mid-pagination failures. page first fails (e.g., HTTP 504 retry budget exhausted), package longer discards rows already fetched. Instead logs warning returns partial tibble attr(result, "partial") = TRUE attr(result, "last_page_error") describing failure. matters CUSTOS, backend timeout caused users lose hundreds rows successful first page page two stalled. CUSTOS default page_size lowered 500 (1000). CUSTOS load balancer became aggressive cutting slow queries; 1000 rows often times broad queries even month org filters. 500 robust starting point cost extra round-trips. actionable HTTP 504 error message CUSTOS — explicitly suggests adding mes filter /reducing page_size, reminds user partial results now available via attributes. README’s CUSTOS quick-start example now passes month filter copy-paste users hit 504 ceiling.

title: tesouror 0.2.0
text: Fault-tolerant batch fetchers SICONFI. New helpers get_rreo_for_state(), get_dca_for_state(), get_rgf_for_state() (plus English aliases get_budget_report_for_state(), get_annual_accounts_for_state(), get_fiscal_report_for_state()) fetch data every municipality Brazilian state single call. loop underlying SICONFI endpoint per municipality tolerate per-entity failures: individual call recovered five-attempt retry budget, failure recorded loop continues. Failed calls returned via attr(result, "failed") (tibble iteration, id, error). on_error argument controls behaviour: "warn" (default), "stop", "silent". addresses fault-tolerance missing-municipality concerns reported rsiconfi#2 rsiconfi#3. Distinct reporting empty responses. SICONFI API replies HTTP 200 zero rows entity homologated given report (common cause “missing municipalities” complaints). new batch fetchers expose cases via attr(result, "no_data") (tibble iteration, id), separate technical failures attr(result, "failed"). Layout-aware RREO tidy layer. New functions rreo_layout(), rreo_normalize_columns(), tidy_rreo() reconcile SICONFI’s drifting column account labels across fiscal years. bundled inst/extdata/rreo_layout.csv knows , example, federal RGPS appendix moved RREO-Anexo 04.3 - RGPS (≤2022) RREO-Anexo 04.4 - RGPS (2023+) account names shifting Roman numerals ("... (VII) = (III - VI)") matched stable stem. rreo_normalize_columns() adds coluna_padrao (year-stripped) coluna_ano (year appeared suffix, NA), letting users distinguish current-year column comparative previous-year column. tidy_rreo(data, topic = "previdencia") returns year-stable indicator column ready longitudinal analysis, collapsing manual paste0(... ifelse(year >= 2021, "", " / "), ...) workarounds workhorse issue rsiconfi#4. Currently ships rules federal previdência (RGPS, RPPS civis, FCDF, militares); contributions additional topics welcome. See new vignette vignette("rreo-longitudinal") end--end walkthrough. New dependency: stringi (>= 1.7.0) (portable diacritic stripping; already transitive dependency via stringr).

title: tesouror 0.2.0
text: Added hard Depends: R (>= 4.1.0) reflect use native pipe (|>) inside package. UF abbreviation guard Transferencias. get_tc_municipios(), get_tc_por_estados(), get_tc_por_estados_detalhe(), get_tc_por_municipio(), get_tc_por_municipio_detalhe() now abort actionable error two-letter Brazilian UF abbreviation (e.g., "PE") passed numeric Treasury state code expected. Previously upstream API returned HTTP 500 long retry budget; new check fires network call points user get_tc_estados().

title: tesouror 0.1.0
text: tesouror expands former siconfir package unified interface Brazilian National Treasury open data APIs.

title: tesouror 0.1.0
text: SICONFI — Fiscal reports (RREO, RGF, DCA, MSC) entity data (functions original siconfir package). CUSTOS — Federal government cost data (active/retired staff, pensioners, depreciation, transfers, costs). SADIPEM — Public debt verification letters (PVL), credit operations, payment schedules, debt capacity. Transferências Constitucionais — Constitutional transfers states municipalities (FPE, FPM, FUNDEB, etc.). SIORG — Federal organizational structure (organs, entities, departments). SIORG codes serve dictionary values organizacao_n1/n2/n3 parameters CUSTOS API. SIOPE — Education spending data FNDE/MEC (general data, revenues, expenses, indicators, compensation). Uses FNDE’s OData-style API.

title: tesouror 0.1.0
text: Bilingual interface: every function Portuguese (API-native) English-named versions English parameter names. -memory caching across APIs (tesouror_clear_cache()). Automatic pagination ORDS-style responses (SICONFI, CUSTOS, SADIPEM) OData-style responses (SIOPE), per-page progress reporting. page_size parameter control rows per page (CUSTOS defaults 1000 instead server’s slow default 250; SIOPE defaults 1000). max_rows parameter paginated functions cap number rows returned. Automatically adjusts limit/$top avoid fetching unnecessary data. Retry logic: 5 attempts progressive backoff (3s, 6s, 9s, 12s, 15s) HTTP 500/502/503/504/429 connection failures. HTTP 400/404 retried produce actionable error messages. verbose parameter every function print full API URL debugging testing browser/curl. Can set globally options(tesouror.verbose = TRUE). janitor::clean_names() applied responses consistent snake_case column names. CUSTOS: SIORG codes auto-padded (244 → "000244"). SIOPE: OData $filter, $orderby, $select support server-side filtering, sorting, column selection. Transferências: multi-value parameters accept R vectors (c(1, 2, 3)) addition colon-separated strings ("1:2:3"). Friendly error messages retry logic transient failures. HTTP 400 errors parse server messages suggest checking column names. NA validation required parameters. Tidy tibble output whitespace trimming.
